items.text:
Notice: Scheduled Water Shutoff — Lawson Residence Hall

The water supply to Lawson Residence Hall will be shut off on Tuesday, June 16, from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM while crews replace aging pipes on the third floor. Residents should prepare for the interruption in advance.

Please observe the following guidelines:
- Store drinking water the night before, as taps will not function during the shutoff.
- Restrooms on the ground floor of the adjacent Pike Center will remain open for resident use.
- Do not run washing machines or dishwashers until service is restored.

Bottled water will be distributed free of charge in the lobby beginning at 8:30 AM. A limited number of shower passes for the campus recreation center are available at the front desk for a $3 deposit, refunded when the pass is returned.

Repairs are expected to finish by 3:00 PM, but residents will be notified by email if the timeline changes. Questions may be directed to the Housing Office at extension 2200.

format_metadata.title: Scheduled Water Shutoff — Lawson Residence Hall

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will be provided free of charge in the lobby?

options.A: Shower passes for the recreation center

questions.explanation: The notice states that bottled water will be distributed free of charge in the lobby.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about residents who need to shower during the shutoff?

options.A: They can use the campus recreation center.
options.B: They must wait until service is restored.
options.C: They should leave campus for the day.
options.D: They will be charged a cleaning fee.

questions.explanation: Shower passes for the campus recreation center are offered, implying residents can shower there while the water is off.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a permanent change to water service
options.B: To inform residents about a temporary water shutoff
options.C: To advertise the campus recreation center
options.D: To describe recent repairs to the third floor

questions.explanation: The notice's purpose is to inform residents about a scheduled, temporary water shutoff and how to prepare.

items.text:
Subject: Study Group for the BIOL 240 Final

Hi everyone,

I'm putting together a study group to prepare for the BIOL 240 final exam on Thursday, December 12. If you've been struggling with the metabolism and genetics units, this is a great chance to review together before the test.

We'll meet twice a week in the Science Library, Group Room 3, starting Monday, November 24. Sessions run from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM. My plan is to work through past quizzes on Mondays and review lecture notes on Wednesdays.

A few things to keep in mind:
- Bring your textbook and a copy of the study guide Professor Nolan posted online.
- We'll split a $20 charge for printed practice exams, so about $4 each if six people join.
- Space is limited to eight students, so reply by Friday, November 21 to reserve a spot.

Let me know which units you'd most like to focus on. I'm hoping we can fill the group quickly.

Thanks,
Daniel Ortega

format_metadata.subject: Study Group for the BIOL 240 Final

questions.stem: According to the email, by when should students reply to reserve a spot?
questions.explanation: The email asks students to reply by Friday, November 21 to reserve a spot.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the cost of the printed practice exams?

options.A: It will be paid in full by Daniel Ortega.
options.B: It will be split among the students who join.
options.C: It is covered entirely by the department.
options.D: It must be paid before the first session.

questions.explanation: The email says the $20 charge will be split so each person pays about $4, implying the cost is shared among those who join.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To announce a change to the final exam date
options.B: To ask students to submit their study guides
options.C: To advertise a paid tutoring service
options.D: To invite students to join a study group

questions.explanation: The email's purpose is to invite classmates to join a study group for the BIOL 240 final.
